title: papaver × pseudo-orientale
intro: Papaver × pseudo-orientale is a hybrid plant species in the poppy family, derived from the cross between Papaver orientale and another species of poppy. The plant contains various alkaloids, including thebaine, codeine, and morphine, which exhibit analgesic, antitussive, and antidiarrheal properties. Medicinally, Papaver × pseudo-orientale has been used to treat coughs, diarrhea, and pain, as well as to induce sleep. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation and alleviate various health issues.
